title: Melicope fatraina
intro: Melicope fatraina, also known as the Fatu-Hiva melicope, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Marquesas Islands in French Polynesia. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief for various health concerns. The bioactive constituents of Melicope fatraina include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.
taxonomy: Melicope fatraina is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a member of the genus Melicope, which contains various species of trees and shrubs native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is commonly found in the wild, particularly in the forests of the Cook Islands. Melicope fatraina is a part of the order Sapindales, which consists of various families of flowering plants. The plant's classification is further defined by its placement in the clade Rosidae, a group of flowering plants that are thought to have originated from a common ancestor.
